heading: Incentives for natural gas production from deep wells in the shallow waters of the Gulf of Mexico
label: 15904 Incentives for natural gas production from deep wells in the shallow waters of the Gulf of Mexico

children.heading: Royalty incentive regulations for ultra deep gas wells
children.label: a Royalty incentive regulations for ultra deep gas wells

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than 180 days after , in addition to any other regulations that may provide royalty incentives for natural gas produced from deep wells on oil and gas leases issued pursuant to the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act ( et seq.), the Secretary shall issue regulations granting royalty relief suspension volumes of not less than 35 billion cubic feet with respect to the production of natural gas from ultra deep wells on leases issued in shallow waters less than 400 meters deep located in the Gulf of Mexico wholly west of 87 degrees, 30 minutes west longitude. Regulations issued under this subsection shall be retroactive to the date that the notice of proposed rulemaking is published in the Federal Register.August 8, 200543 U.S.C. 1331

children.label: A
children.text: the ultra deep well is a sidetrack; or

children.label: B
children.text: the lease has previously produced from wells with a perforated interval the top of which is at least 15,000 feet true vertical depth below the datum at mean sea level.

children.heading: Ultra deep well
children.label: A Ultra deep well
children.text: The term “ultra deep well” means a well drilled with a perforated interval, the top of which is at least 20,000 true vertical depth below the datum at mean sea level.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The term “sidetrack” means a well resulting from drilling an additional hole to a new objective bottom-hole location by leaving a previously drilled hole.

children.label: I
children.text: drilling a well from a platform slot reclaimed from a previously drilled well;

children.label: II
children.text: re-entering and deepening a previously drilled well; and

children.label: III
children.text: a bypass from a sidetrack, including drilling around material blocking a hole or drilling to straighten a crooked hole.

children.heading: Royalty incentive regulations for deep gas wells
children.label: b Royalty incentive regulations for deep gas wells
children.text: Not later than 180 days after , in addition to any other regulations that may provide royalty incentives for natural gas produced from deep wells on oil and gas leases issued pursuant to the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act ( et seq.), the Secretary shall issue regulations granting royalty relief suspension volumes with respect to production of natural gas from deep wells on leases issued in waters more than 200 meters but less than 400 meters deep located in the Gulf of Mexico wholly west of 87 degrees, 30 minutes west longitude. The suspension volumes for deep wells within 200 to 400 meters of water depth shall be calculated using the same methodology used to calculate the suspension volumes for deep wells in the shallower waters of the Gulf of Mexico, and in no case shall the suspension volumes for deep wells within 200 to 400 meters of water depth be lower than those for deep wells in shallower waters. Regulations issued under this subsection shall be retroactive to the date that the notice of proposed rulemaking is published in the Federal Register.August 8, 200543 U.S.C. 1331

children.heading: Limitations
children.label: c Limitations
children.text: The Secretary may place limitations on the royalty relief granted under this section based on market price. The royalty relief granted under this section shall not apply to a lease for which deep water royalty relief is available.
